result.summary: Google Analytics exposes two authenticated HTTP APIs (Data API and Admin API) and a Google Analytics MCP server; auth is via Google OAuth/access tokens or service account credentials, while MCP auth could not be pinned from the docs.

google_oauth.label: Google OAuth 2.0 access token
google_oauth.setup: Create or select a Google Cloud project, enable the Google Analytics Data API and/or Admin API, then configure an OAuth consent screen and OAuth client in [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials). For local use, the quickest path is to run the Google client library / sample app sign-in flow and let it mint an OAuth access token for your Google account. The token is sent as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` to the Analytics APIs. The same Google account must have access to the Analytics property/account you want to query.

google_service_account.label: Google service account credentials
google_service_account.setup: Create a service account in [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/iam-admin/serviceaccounts), grant it access to the relevant Google Analytics property or account, and download the JSON key if you need a private key for JWT-based auth. The Analytics client libraries can also use application default credentials without you manually handling the JWT. This is the server-to-server path used when you don't want a user OAuth flow.

surfaces.name: Google Analytics MCP server
surfaces.notes: Local MCP server installed from the GitHub repo; no hosted endpoint.
